task_completion.description: Verify that the agent logged in as admin and accessed the BIO101 course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user profile indicates an admin user is logged in. The page header, breadcrumbs, or navigation menu shows 'Introduction to Biology' or 'BIO101'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Database activity named 'Medication Reference Database' was created with the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The course page or activity settings page shows a Database activity named 'Medication Reference Database'. The description 'A collaborative reference of medications encountered during clinical rotations' is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Drug Name', 'Indications', and 'Common Side Effects' fields were created with the correct types.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the database fields list, 'Drug Name' is listed as a 'Text input' (or short text), while 'Indications' and 'Common Side Effects' are listed as 'Text area' (or long text).

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Drug Class' field was created as a menu/dropdown.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the database fields list, 'Drug Class' is listed as a 'Menu' or dropdown field type. If visible, the options should include Analgesic, Antibiotic, Antihypertensive, Anticoagulant, and Other.

task_completion.description: Verify that a sample entry was added for Amoxicillin with the correct drug class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the database 'View list', 'View single', or 'Add entry' tab, there is an entry with 'Amoxicillin' entered for the Drug Name and 'Antibiotic' selected for the Drug Class.

task_completion.description: Verify that the sample entry includes the correct indications and side effects.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the same database entry, the Indications field contains 'Bacterial infections including ear infections, pneumonia, and urinary tract infections' and the Common Side Effects field contains 'Nausea, diarrhea, skin rash'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a real Moodle LMS interface in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface navigating a Moodle LMS, not a mock-up or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web GUI to create the database and fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent filling out Moodle forms, clicking buttons, and navigating the Moodle UI, rather than running backend scripts or terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just write a script to output the final expected text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows step-by-step creation of the activity, fields, and entry within the Moodle interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target course is 'Introduction to Biology' (shortname: BIO101). The expected activity name is 'Medication Reference Database'. The expected fields are 'Drug Name' (text), 'Drug Class' (menu), 'Indications' (textarea), and 'Common Side Effects' (textarea). The menu options for Drug Class are Analgesic, Antibiotic, Antihypertensive, Anticoagulant, Other. The expected sample entry is for 'Amoxicillin' (Drug Class: Antibiotic) with indications 'Bacterial infections including middle/ear infections, pneumonia, and urinary tract infections' and side effects 'Nausea, diarrhea, skin rash'.
